key_insight: The mean inner potential (MIP) of alloyed and strained materials can be modeled accurately by combining a linear concentration dependence at constant volume with a volume-rescaling correction when lattice parameters change.
engineering_relevance: This provides a practical DFT-based rule for predicting electrostatic potential shifts in alloys and strained crystals, useful for electron microscopy contrast interpretation and materials design.
method: DFT calculations with an augmented plane waves plus local orbitals basis set
Tested alloy systems with and without significant lattice-parameter change
Added volume-scaling to capture strain- and density-driven MIP variations
evidence_signal: The abstract reports linear MIP–concentration behavior for AlGaAs and clear bowing for InGaP and GeSi, with the bowing reproduced by including volume rescaling.
watch_item: The model appears most reliable when the volume change is explicitly accounted for; ignoring lattice expansion/compression can lead to incorrect MIP trends.
paper_title: Modelling the mean inner potential of alloyed and strained materials
